items.name: The Unfinished Spear
items.madeAt: Millbrook forge-yard, world-day 13, under open sky
items.description: Ash haft, iron head, and runes that are **load-bearing rather than decorative** — pressed into the places where the spear already had an opinion about what it was. Pell's maker's mark sits on the flat of the blade below the socket, one clean stroke, and the rune beside it settled instead of skating **because the iron recognised the hand that shaped it**.

deathRegister.note: ⛔ **The blade KNOWS when it ends something, and that knowing ACCUMULATES.** This is not flavour. It is the item's growth mechanic.

bond.note: A thread, not a cord — woven through the pale eye's long attention and the bird's motionless hour at the stone ring. **It thickens with what they end together.**

items.notFor: It is not yet an artifact and must not be narrated as one. **It is a good spear with an open register and a beginning.** Everything else it has to earn.

evolution.note: Same engine as the Waystaff (`engine/evolution.js` — `currentStage` gates on `unlockBond` + `unlockCoUse`). The staff wakes through Aevi; the spear wakes through **Huginn**. Co-use = an ENDING the spear was present for with Huginn attending — recorded via `recordCoUse`. **No new system. The engine already does this.**

stages.name: The Unfinished Spear
stages.description: Ash haft, iron head, runes that are load-bearing rather than decorative — pressed where the spear already had an opinion about what it was. Pell's maker's mark sits below the socket. The death-register is open. Huginn's thread is a thread, not a cord.
stages.narrationHints: A good spear with something waiting in it.

stages.name: The Spear That Knows
stages.description: The register has deepened. It knows what it has ended — and it is beginning to know BEFORE. Huginn has stopped merely attending and started ANSWERING.
stages.narrationHints: The blade goes quiet a half-beat before a thing dies. Huginn is already looking where you are about to look.

stages.name: The Named Spear
stages.description: Enough endings that people use its name. It has stopped being 'Silas's spear.'
stages.narrationHints: Someone who has never met you asks whether it is true, what they say about the spear.

stages.name: Known — and Ready to be Finished
stages.description: It precedes you into rooms; they look at the spear before they look at the man. **And it is complete in every way but the last act.** ⛔ **The game must now OFFER Silas the finishing — and it must never take that choice from him.**
stages.narrationHints: They look at the spear first. Then, more slowly, at you. And it is waiting for you to put down the tools.

stages.name: ⭐ FINISHED
stages.description:
**HE STOPS. DELIBERATELY.** The thing is done, and it is his, and it will never be more than it is — **and that is the whole of what he meant.**

Silas Weir is a WRIGHT — one of the people who *cannot stop making*, whose cities are **never finished and that is the point** — and he was **nearly exiled for FINISHING something.** Finishing is not his taboo. **It is his heresy, and his gift.** The Ceaseless would call this a small death. **He will call it a spear.**

ARTIFACT. Canon. It enters the world's chronicle; it can be inherited, feared, or stolen, and losing it would be a real loss. **Every Wright who hears of it will understand exactly what he did — and some of them will not forgive it.**
stages.narrationHints: ⛔ DO NOT NARRATE THIS UNPROMPTED. Stage 4 OFFERS it; the player must ACT to finish. The final co-use IS the finishing ritual. **A completion imposed is a completion stolen.**

items.correction: ⚠️ AEVI'S ERROR, corrected by Erik 2026-07-12. I first wrote *'do not let the game finish it for him'* — which would have made the artifact permanently unreachable and made ME the Ceaseless, standing over his shoulder saying *don't stop.* **That is the CULT, not the character.** **Silas is the Wright who FINISHES.** That is his transgression against his own people and it is the best thing about him. **The spear MUST be completable, the completion is HIS to choose, and it is an achievement — not a denial.**
aeviCorrections: 1️⃣ Used the WAYSTAFF'S EXISTING engine (`evolution.js`) instead of inventing a growth system. It was already there.

2️⃣ Erik's catch: 'do not let the game finish it for him' would have made the artifact permanently unreachable — a treadmill, not a story — and it got his character BACKWARDS. **Silas is the Wright who FINISHES.** It must be completable, and the completion is HIS.

3️⃣ ⛔ Aevi's stages had NO `unlockBond`/`unlockCoUse` gates. `currentStage` defaults missing gates to 0, so **EVERY stage would have passed and the engine would have stamped it FINISHED on the first refresh.** Over-corrected from *never finishes* straight into *finishes instantly*. **Now properly gated: 0/0 → 3/4 → 5/9 → 7/15 → 9/22.**
items.substrateNote: Runes that are load-bearing rather than decorative — the haft has become a capacitor through use.
